3 And let us bring again the ark of our God to us; for we inquired not at it in the days of Saul.
[SHALT THOU BRING INTO THE ARK.] This is a command to Noah not to desert these creatures but to bring them into the ark with him in order to preserve the seed of each species.

7 And they carried the ark of God in a new.cart: out: of the: house of Abinadab ; and Uzza and Ahic drave the cart. ©
7 And they carried the ark of God in a new.cart: out: of the: house of Abinadab ; and Uzza and Ahic drave the cart. ©

up the ark of God from the house of Abinadab that was in Gibeah, they put it upon a new cart, and Uzzah and Ahio the sons of Abinadab drove the new cart. When they came to Nachon's threshing floor, Uzzah put forth his hand to the ark of God, and took hold of it, for the oxen shook it. The anger of the Lord was kindled against Uzzah, and God smote him there for his error; and there he died by the ark of God. Let the kingdom alone, the Lord steadies the ark; and if it does jostle, and appear to…

that day, saying, How shall I bring the ark of God home to me? - 13.80 David brought. not the ark home to himself to the city of David, but carried it aside into the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
9 And David was afraid of the Lord that day, and said, How shall the ark of the Lord come to me?
that day, saying, How shall I bring the ark of God home to me? - 13.80 David brought. not the ark home to himself to the city of David, but carried it aside into the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
that day, saying, How shall I bring the ark of God home to me? - 13.80 David brought. not the ark home to himself to the city of David, but carried it aside into the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
From all living things, from all flesh, two of each shall you bring to the ark, to keep them alive with you. They shall be male and female.

From every living being of all flesh, two of each you shall bring to the ark to keep alive with you; they shall be male and female. You must bring into the ark the living beings that will form the basis of the future existence of both mankind and the animal kingdom.
[SHALT THOU BRING INTO THE ARK.] This is a command to Noah not to desert these creatures but to bring them into the ark with him in order to preserve the seed of each species.
that day, saying, How shall I bring the ark of God home to me? - 13.80 David brought. not the ark home to himself to the city of David, but carried it aside into the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.

Perhaps this psalm was composed on the same occasion as the 24th and 47th, when David brought up the ark of God to that tabernacle, which he had pitched for it in Zion, 2 Sam. 6.
Whether this psalm was penned when David brought up the ark of God to Mount Zion, 2Sam. 6, or relates to the Chaldean captivity, is uncertain.
Probably this psalm was penned for use of the Hebrews, when David brought up the ark of God to Jerusalem, or when Solomon brought it into the temple, 2 Sam. 6, 1 Kings 8.
